name: Pressure Vessel Thickness Calculator
description: Calculates minimum required wall thickness for cylindrical pressure vessels based on ASME BPVC Section VIII Div. 1 standards, with integrated safety factors, material utilization, and cost optimization.

standardOptions.label: ASME BPVC Section VIII
standardOptions.description: American Society of Mechanical Engineers Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code

inputs.label: Design Pressure (P)
inputs.businessContext: Maximum allowable working pressure per ASME UG-21

businessContext_i18n.en: Maximum allowable working pressure per ASME UG-21

inputs.label: Allowable Stress (S)
inputs.businessContext: Material allowable stress at design temperature per ASME II Part D

businessContext_i18n.en: Material allowable stress at design temperature per ASME II Part D

inputs.label: Joint Efficiency (E)
inputs.businessContext: Welded joint efficiency factor per ASME UW-12

businessContext_i18n.en: Welded joint efficiency factor per ASME UW-12

inputs.label: Corrosion Allowance (CA)
inputs.businessContext: Additional thickness for corrosion/erosion per UG-25

businessContext_i18n.en: Additional thickness for corrosion/erosion per UG-25

inputs.label: Material Utilization Factor
inputs.businessContext: Lean manufacturing metric for material waste reduction

businessContext_i18n.en: Lean manufacturing metric for material waste reduction

inputs.label: Override Safety Factor
inputs.businessContext: Enable to use custom safety factor instead of ASME default 3.5

businessContext_i18n.en: Enable to use custom safety factor instead of ASME default 3.5

inputs.label: Custom Safety Factor
inputs.businessContext: Only used if override is enabled; typical range per ISO 16528

businessContext_i18n.en: Only used if override is enabled; typical range per ISO 16528

rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 38.5% of allowable stress. Thick-wall formulas required (ASME UG-27(c)(2)).
Diameter-to-thickness ratio < 10. Consider thick-wall vessel analysis per ASME Appendix 1.
Material utilization below 70%. Review nesting and cutting plan for Lean improvement.

thresholds.message: Calculated thickness < 5mm. Fabrication handling risk; consider minimum thickness per ASME UG-16.
Material cost exceeds $150/unit. Evaluate alternative materials or reduce corrosion allowance.

formulas.name: Effective Safety Factor
formulas.description: Determines the safety factor based on override flag

formulas.name: Minimum Required Thickness (Cylindrical Shell)
formulas.description: ASME UG-27 thin-wall formula for cylindrical shells under internal pressure

formulas.name: Thickness Including Corrosion Allowance
formulas.description: Adds corrosion allowance to the calculated minimum thickness

formulas.name: Material Volume per Unit Length
formulas.description: Volume of material required per meter of vessel length

formulas.name: Material Cost per Unit Length
formulas.description: Cost based on material volume and utilization factor

formulas.name: Weight per Meter
formulas.description: Weight of vessel shell per meter length

formulas.name: Recommended Design Thickness
formulas.description: Final recommended thickness rounded up to nearest 0.5mm for manufacturability

primary.label: Recommended Design Thickness
primary.description: Final thickness rounded to nearest 0.5mm for fabrication

material_volume_per_m.label: Material Volume per Meter
material_volume_per_m.description: Volume of steel per unit length

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excess Corrosion Allowance
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Corrosion allowance > 6mm adds unnecessary weight and cost without proportional life extension

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Joint Efficiency
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Using spot or no radiography increases required thickness by 15-40% vs full radiography

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Material Utilization Waste
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Utilization below 85% indicates poor nesting or cutting optimization

suggestedActions.label: Consider Full Radiography
suggestedActions.description: If joint efficiency is below 1.0, evaluate cost-benefit of full radiography to reduce thickness
suggestedActions.impact: Reduces required thickness by up to 30%

suggestedActions.label: Review Corrosion Allowance
suggestedActions.description: If corrosion rate is <0.1mm/year, consider reducing CA to 1.5mm

suggestedActions.label: Improve Nesting Efficiency
suggestedActions.description: Use advanced nesting software to increase material utilization above 90%

suggestedActions.label: Evaluate Higher Strength Material
suggestedActions.description: If allowable stress < 200 MPa, consider SA-516 Gr.70 or equivalent
